description: Project future values by shifting data forward in time

variations.name: Project next week's inventory needs
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lead(
    columns=['stock_level', 'sales_rate'],
    periods=7,
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['warehouse_id', 'product_id'],
    suffix='_projected'
)

variations.name: Forecast product demand for next 14 days
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lead(
    columns=['demand', 'orders_count'],
    periods=14,
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['product_category', 'region'],
    suffix='_forecast'
)

variations.name: Project user acquisition for next month
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lead(
    columns=['new_users', 'conversion_rate'],
    periods=30,
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['acquisition_channel'],
    suffix='_projected'
)

variations.name: Estimate next day's web traffic by hour
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lead(
    columns=['visitors', 'page_views', 'server_load'],
    periods=24,
    time_column='timestamp',
    group_columns=['website_section'],
    suffix='_forecast'
)

description: Compare current values with previous time periods

variations.name: Compare current sales with previous week
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lag(
    columns=['daily_sales', 'transaction_count', 'average_basket'],
    periods=7,
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['store_id'],
    suffix='_previous_week'
)

variations.name: Calculate month-over-month change
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lag(
    columns=['revenue', 'active_users', 'acquisition_cost'],
    periods=1,
    time_column='month',
    group_columns=['business_unit'],
    suffix='_prev_month'
)

variations.name: Compare with performance from 30 days ago
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lag(
    columns=['visitors', 'bounce_rate', 'conversion_rate', 'page_views'],
    periods=30,
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['page_category'],
    suffix='_30d_prior'
)

variations.name: Compare quarterly results year-over-year
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | lag(
    columns=['revenue', 'expenses', 'profit', 'customer_count'],
    periods=4,
    time_column='quarter',
    group_columns=['region'],
    suffix='_yoy'
)

description: Analyze volatility and variability in time series data

variations.name: Analyze daily traffic volatility with rolling window
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | variance_analysis(
    columns=['visitors', 'page_views', 'conversion_rate'],
    method='rolling',
    window=7,
    time_column='date',
    suffix='_volatility'
)

variations.name: Calculate expanding standard deviation
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | variance_analysis(
    columns=['sales', 'units_sold'],
    method='expanding',
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['product_category'],
    suffix='_std'
)

variations.name: Measure weekly order volume fluctuations
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | variance_analysis(
    columns=['order_count', 'order_value'],
    method='rolling',
    window=4,
    time_column='week',
    group_columns=['region'],
    suffix='_fluctuation'
)

variations.name: Analyze hourly server load variability
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | variance_analysis(
    columns=['cpu_usage', 'memory_usage', 'request_count'],
    method='rolling',
    window=24,
    time_column='timestamp',
    group_columns=['server_id'],
    suffix='_variability'
)

description: Analyze distribution patterns of data across different groups

variations.name: Analyze order value distribution by sales channel
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | distribution_analysis(
    columns=['order_value'],
    bins=10,
    group_columns=['sales_channel'],
    normalize=True
)

variations.name: Analyze page load time distribution by device
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | distribution_analysis(
    columns=['load_time_ms'],
    bins=20,
    group_columns=['device_type'],
    normalize=False
)

variations.name: Create session duration histograms by user segment
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | distribution_analysis(
    columns=['session_duration'],
    bins=15,
    group_columns=['user_segment'],
    normalize=True
)

variations.name: Analyze purchase amount distribution by age group
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | distribution_analysis(
    columns=['purchase_amount'],
    bins=8,
    group_columns=['age_group'],
    normalize=True
)

description: Calculate running totals and cumulative distributions over time

variations.name: Calculate cumulative new customer acquisitions
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | cumulative_distribution(
    columns=['new_customers'],
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['marketing_channel'],
    suffix='_cumulative'
)

variations.name: Track cumulative revenue by product category
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | cumulative_distribution(
    columns=['revenue'],
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['product_category'],
    suffix='_cumulative'
)

variations.name: Generate cumulative user registrations by source
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | cumulative_distribution(
    columns=['new_registrations'],
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['acquisition_source'],
    suffix='_cumulative'
)

variations.name: Calculate cumulative spend vs budget year-to-date
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | cumulative_distribution(
    columns=['spend', 'budget'],
    time_column='date',
    group_columns=['department'],
    suffix='_ytd'
)

description: Apply custom calculations and transformations to time series data

variations.name: Apply seasonality adjustment to sales data
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | custom_calculation(
    lambda df: df.assign(sales_adjusted=df['sales'] / df['seasonal_factor'])
)

variations.name: Calculate MACD for sales trends
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | custom_calculation(
    lambda df: df.assign(
        ema12=df['sales'].ewm(span=12).mean(),
        ema26=df['sales'].ewm(span=26).mean()
    ).assign(macd=lambda d: d['ema12'] - d['ema26'])
)

variations.name: Apply logarithmic transformation to skewed data
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | custom_calculation(
    lambda df: df.assign(log_sales=np.log1p(df['sales']))
)

variations.name: Calculate growth velocity and acceleration
variations.example:
ts_pipe = TimeSeriesPipe.from_dataframe(data)
ts_pipe = ts_pipe | custom_calculation(
    lambda df: df.assign(
        growth_velocity=df['metric'].diff(),
        growth_acceleration=df['metric'].diff().diff()
    )
)

variations.name: Apply custom weighting to combine metrics
